root_motivation: The self-review loop rediscovers the same system findings every run because there is no per-finding primary key; without a key there is no deduplication, streak tracking, escalation threshold, or auto-close — docket-core provides that key and the minimal CRUD surface that downstream automation (docket-escalate, docket-self-review-bind) depends on.
user_persona: Claude Code running self-review on jsy's laptop; also jsy manually inspecting open findings via the CLI.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | grep -cE '^test acceptance_[a-z0-9_]+ \.\.\. ok' || true

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build --release produces target/release/docket; --version prints 'docket <semver>' and --help lists report|list|show|resolve subcommands.

docket report --run r1 --key k --title T on empty store creates row with status=open, first_seen==last_seen, runs_seen=1, consecutive_runs=1, report_count=1 (verified via docket show k --format json).
Reporting key k again with same --run r1 leaves runs_seen=1 and consecutive_runs=1 but sets report_count=2 and bumps last_seen.
Reporting key k with new --run r2 sets runs_seen=2, consecutive_runs=2, last_run=r2.

docket list --open --format json returns JSON array containing k; after docket resolve k --reason done, docket list --open omits k, docket list --resolved includes it with resolve_reason=done and non-null resolved_at.
Reporting a resolved key reopens it: status=open, consecutive_runs=1, resolved_at null again.
docket show <unknown-key> exits nonzero with a clear message; docket list on empty store exits 0 with empty output/[].
DB is created at XDG path on first use without manual mkdir; a second invocation reuses it (report in one process, list in another, no data loss).
--format json output for list and show is valid JSON parseable by python3 -m json.tool / jq .
README documents the schema, run model, and every subcommand with a worked example (report -> list -> show -> resolve).

scope: report subcommand: upsert a finding by key, track runs_seen/consecutive_runs/report_count
list subcommand: filter by status (open/resolved/all), severity, format (text/json)
show subcommand: display full finding record, nonzero exit on unknown key
resolve subcommand: mark finding resolved with optional reason
WAL mode + BEGIN IMMEDIATE for safe concurrent report calls
Reopen semantics: resolved finding reported again becomes open with streak reset to 1
non_goals: Typed evidence references and trail rendering (docket-evidence PRD)
Gap detection in consecutive_runs (run-id must be monotonic; gap logic is docket-escalate)

additional.no_let_chains: MSRV 1.85 predates let-chains; do not use
additional.bundled_sqlite: rusqlite with bundled feature to avoid system SQLite dep

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a structured finding ledger at all?
five_whys_trace.a: The self-review skill rediscovers identical findings (e.g. 'agorabus stale binary') every run with no deduplication — the same finding appears 7 times in a single journal with no shared identity.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does lacking a shared identity cause a real problem?
five_whys_trace.a: Without a primary key there is no streak counter, so you cannot detect 'this finding has persisted for 3+ consecutive runs' and trigger escalation — the core signal for 'this needs owner action'.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not encode that in recall memories or free-text journal sections?
five_whys_trace.a: recall memories are prose blobs with no queryable fields; free-text journal sections have no cross-run identity. You cannot issue 'show me all findings surviving 3+ runs' against unstructured text.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must it be a CLI tool rather than a library used internally by self-review?
five_whys_trace.a: Other skills (self-review, docket-escalate, docket-self-review-bind) need to call it from bash via subprocess; a CLI with a stable verb contract is the natural IPC boundary for shell-level automation.

five_whys_trace.q: Why SQLite rather than a plain JSONL file?
five_whys_trace.a: Concurrent report calls from overlapping self-review iterations would corrupt a JSONL file; SQLite WAL + BEGIN IMMEDIATE provides the atomic upsert needed for correct streak counting without a daemon.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should edition be 2024 or 2021?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: 2021 — MSRV 1.85 supports 2024 edition but the PRD explicitly references the recall baseline constraint (no let-chains, MSRV 1.85, edition 2021).

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is --evidence flag stored or parsed?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Stored as raw TEXT string in v1; typed parsing is docket-evidence. Accept the flag now so binding PRD call sites are stable.
